title: ST 558 Project 1
text:
Before we can begin writing our helper function to query the census API, we need to understand the structure of its return. After obtaining an API key, we can use one of the provided example URLs:

library(httr)

Warning: package 'httr' was built under R version 4.5.3

api_key <- "59c3be738e0d051c63b57b6327de0b11ef0f7a1e"

base_url <- paste0("https://api.census.gov/data/2022/acs/acs1/pums?get=SEX,PWGTP,MAR&for=state:*&SCHL=24&key=",
                   api_key)

api_return <- GET(base_url)

str(api_return)

List of 10
 $ url        : chr "https://api.census.gov/data/2022/acs/acs1/pums?get=SEX,PWGTP,MAR&for=state:*&SCHL=24&key=59c3be738e0d051c63b57b"| __truncated__
 $ status_code: int 200
 $ headers    :List of 12
  ..$ cache-control               : chr "max-age=60, must-revalidate"
  ..$ access-control-allow-origin : chr "*"
  ..$ access-control-allow-methods: chr "GET"
  ..$ access-control-allow-headers: chr "Origin, X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Accept"
  ..$ referrer-policy             : chr "same-origin"
  ..$ content-security-policy     : chr "default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self"| __truncated__
  ..$ x-content-type-options      : chr "nosniff"
  ..$ content-type                : chr "application/json;charset=utf-8"
  ..$ date                        : chr "Tue, 16 Jun 2026 16:45:49 GMT"
  ..$ strict-transport-security   : chr "max-age=31536000"
  ..$ set-cookie                  : chr "TS010383f0=01283c52a45ada6ec1e1a05329a100de942358267f3e4ecf9a357c2a3080ac3d5667649f9b105fd52c3c3d963709dc297a10"| __truncated__
  ..$ transfer-encoding           : chr "chunked"
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "insensitive" "list"
 $ all_headers:List of 1
  ..$ :List of 3
  .. ..$ status : int 200
  .. ..$ version: chr "HTTP/1.1"
  .. ..$ headers:List of 12
  .. .. ..$ cache-control               : chr "max-age=60, must-revalidate"
  .. .. ..$ access-control-allow-origin : chr "*"
  .. .. ..$ access-control-allow-methods: chr "GET"
  .. .. ..$ access-control-allow-headers: chr "Origin, X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Accept"
  .. .. ..$ referrer-policy             : chr "same-origin"
  .. .. ..$ content-security-policy     : chr "default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self"| __truncated__
  .. .. ..$ x-content-type-options      : chr "nosniff"
  .. .. ..$ content-type                : chr "application/json;charset=utf-8"
  .. .. ..$ date                        : chr "Tue, 16 Jun 2026 16:45:49 GMT"
  .. .. ..$ strict-transport-security   : chr "max-age=31536000"
  .. .. ..$ set-cookie                  : chr "TS010383f0=01283c52a45ada6ec1e1a05329a100de942358267f3e4ecf9a357c2a3080ac3d5667649f9b105fd52c3c3d963709dc297a10"| __truncated__
  .. .. ..$ transfer-encoding           : chr "chunked"
  .. .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "insensitive" "list"
 $ cookies    :'data.frame':    1 obs. of  7 variables:
  ..$ domain    : chr "#HttpOnly_.api.census.gov"
  ..$ flag      : logi TRUE
  ..$ path      : chr "/"
  ..$ secure    : logi TRUE
  ..$ expiration: POSIXct[1:1], format: "Inf"
  ..$ name      : chr "TS010383f0"
  ..$ value     : chr "01283c52a45ada6ec1e1a05329a100de942358267f3e4ecf9a357c2a3080ac3d5667649f9b105fd52c3c3d963709dc297a10afbb1c"
 $ content    : raw [1:1157911] 5b 5b 22 53 ...
 $ date       : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-06-16 16:45:49"
 $ times      : Named num [1:6] 0 0.00458 0.1607 0.48095 0.9295 ...
  ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:6] "redirect" "namelookup" "connect" "pretransfer" ...
 $ request    :List of 7
  ..$ method    : chr "GET"
  ..$ url       : chr "https://api.census.gov/data/2022/acs/acs1/pums?get=SEX,PWGTP,MAR&for=state:*&SCHL=24&key=59c3be738e0d051c63b57b"| __truncated__
  ..$ headers   : Named chr "application/json, text/xml, application/xml, */*"
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr "Accept"
  ..$ fields    : NULL
  ..$ options   :List of 2
  .. ..$ useragent: chr "libcurl/8.14.1 r-curl/7.0.0 httr/1.4.8"
  .. ..$ httpget  : logi TRUE
  ..$ auth_token: NULL
  ..$ output    : list()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "write_memory" "write_function"
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "request"
 $ handle     :Class 'curl_handle' <externalptr> 
 - attr(*, "class")= chr "response"


The API call returns a lot of information, but it looks like the stuff we’re interested in lives in the content element. We’ll have to parse the JSON data before we can use it:

suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
library(jsonlite)

Warning: package 'jsonlite' was built under R version 4.5.3



Attaching package: 'jsonlite'


The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':

    flatten

parsed <- fromJSON(rawToChar(api_return$content)) |>
          as_tibble()

Warning: The `x` argument of `as_tibble.matrix()` must have unique column names if
`.name_repair` is omitted as of tibble 2.0.0.
ℹ Using compatibility `.name_repair`.

head(parsed)

# A tibble: 6 × 5
  V1    V2    V3    V4    V5   
  <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr>
1 SEX   PWGTP MAR   SCHL  state
2 2     6     5     24    17   
3 2     23    2     24    06   
4 1     23    3     24    42   
5 1     80    5     24    49   
6 1     16    1     24    18   


After parsing the JSON data and formatting as a tibble, we can see that the first row returned are actually the variable names. We can fix this later.


Now that we understand the type of object the API call returns, I think we’re good to start building the helper function. Building our API call should be straightforward enough (theoretically we just use the paste function), but we need to make sure each input the user provides is valid.


Let’s start by creating a barebones function with the given inputs, defaults, etc:

query.census <- function(year = 2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                         geography = "State", specific_geo = "") {

}

We can validate the user’s inputs one by one, stopping if the function is passed too few options, too many, or invalid ones. We can start by validating the user entered a correct year. They can input any year between 2010 and 2022 inclusive, but not 2020. Adding a check to our function:

query.census <- function(year = 2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                         geography = "State", specific_geo = "") {
  # check for valid year
  if(year == 2020 | !(year %in% 2010:2022)) stop("Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)")
  
  return("API would be called")
}

Let’s make sure the year validation is working as intended. For testing, I just have the function return a message if the input is valid.

query.census()

[1] "API would be called"

query.census(2021)

[1] "API would be called"

query.census(2020)

Error in query.census(2020): Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)

query.census(-2000)

Error in query.census(-2000): Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)


Working as intended! Next is validating the inputs for the numeric and categorical variables.
For numeric variables, we need to consider following:
-The user has to leave the default AGEP or specify their own selection of variables -The additional variables they specify must be valid -PWGTP should always be returned regardless of the user’s selection -There must be at least two numeric variables returned
To satisfy these conditions, we should:

Make sure the user doesn’t pass NULL as their selection
Make sure each variable specified is valid
Add PWGTP to the vector of selected variables

Adding this workflow to our function, we have the following. I’m returning the vector of numeric variables to verify everything is working correctly:

query.census <- function(year = 2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                         geography = "State", specific_geo = "") {
  # check for valid year
  if(year == 2020 | !(year %in% 2010:2022)) stop("Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)")
  
  # check for valid numeric vars
  numeric_vars_opts <- c("AGEP", "GASP", "GRPIP", "JWAP", "JWDP", "JWMNP")
  if(length(numeric_vars) == 0) stop("No numeric variable selected. Specify at least one.")
  for(i in numeric_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% numeric_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid numeric variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: AGEP, GASP, GRPIP, JWAP, JWDP, JWMNP")
  }
  
  numeric_vars <- c(numeric_vars, "PWGTP")
  
  return(numeric_vars)
}

Let’s test:

query.census()

[1] "AGEP"  "PWGTP"

query.census(numeric_vars = NULL)

Error in query.census(numeric_vars = NULL): No numeric variable selected. Specify at least one.

query.census(numeric_vars = "ST 558")

Error in query.census(numeric_vars = "ST 558"): Invalid numeric variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: AGEP, GASP, GRPIP, JWAP, JWDP, JWMNP

query.census(numeric_vars = c("JWDP", "GASP"))

[1] "JWDP"  "GASP"  "PWGTP"


Working as intended! We can do the same thing for the categorical variables:

query.census <- function(year = 2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                         geography = "State", specific_geo = "") {
  # check for valid year
  if(year == 2020 | !(year %in% 2010:2022)) stop("Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)")
  
  # check for valid numeric vars
  numeric_vars_opts <- c("AGEP", "GASP", "GRPIP", "JWAP", "JWDP", "JWMNP")
  if(length(numeric_vars) == 0) stop("No numeric variable selected. Specify at least one.")
  for(i in numeric_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% numeric_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid numeric variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: AGEP, GASP, GRPIP, JWAP, JWDP, JWMNP")
  }
  
  numeric_vars <- c(numeric_vars, "PWGTP")
  
  # check for valid categorical vars
  categorical_vars_opts <- c("FER", "HHL", "SCH", "SCHL", "SEX")
  if(length(categorical_vars) == 0) stop("No categorical variable selected. Specify at least one or leave SEX as the default")
  for(i in categorical_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% categorical_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid categorical variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: FER, HHL, SCH, SCHL, SEX")
  }
  
  return(categorical_vars)
}

More testing:

query.census()

[1] "SEX"

query.census(categorical_vars = c("SEX", "HHL"))

[1] "SEX" "HHL"

query.census(categorical_vars = "ST 558")

Error in query.census(categorical_vars = "ST 558"): Invalid categorical variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: FER, HHL, SCH, SCHL, SEX


Perfect! The last thing to validate is the user’s selection for geography. We have to consider the following:

geography must be State, Region, or District
specific_geo must be between 1 and 50 for state, 1 and 4 for region, and 1 and 9 for division
specific_geo can be “all” for any selection of geography

Adding these considerations to our function:

query.census <- function(year = 2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                         geography = "State", specific_geo = 3) {
  # check for valid year
  if(year == 2020 | !(year %in% 2010:2022)) stop("Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)")
  
  # check for valid numeric vars
  numeric_vars_opts <- c("AGEP", "GASP", "GRPIP", "JWAP", "JWDP", "JWMNP")
  if(length(numeric_vars) == 0) stop("No numeric variable selected. Specify at least one.")
  for(i in numeric_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% numeric_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid numeric variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: AGEP, GASP, GRPIP, JWAP, JWDP, JWMNP")
  }
  
  numeric_vars <- c(numeric_vars, "PWGTP")
  
  # check for valid categorical vars
  categorical_vars_opts <- c("FER", "HHL", "SCH", "SCHL", "SEX")
  if(length(categorical_vars) == 0) stop("No categorical variable selected. Specify at least one or leave SEX as the default")
  for(i in categorical_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% categorical_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid categorical variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: FER, HHL, SCH, SCHL, SEX")
  }
  
  # check for valid geography scope and region of interest
  geography_opts <- c("Region", "State", "Division")
  if(!(geography %in% geography_opts)) stop("Invalid geography classification. Specify one of: State, Region, Division")
  if(specific_geo == "all") {
    specific_geo = "*"
  }
  else {
    if(geography == "State" & !(specific_geo %in% 1:50)) stop("Invalid state requested. Specify a number 1 to 50 or `all`")
    if(geography == "Region" & !(specific_geo %in% 1:4)) stop("Invalid region requested. Specify a number 1 to 4 or `all`")
    if(geography == "Division" & !(specific_geo %in% 1:9)) stop("Invalid division requested. Specify a number 1 to 9 or `all`")
  }
  
  return("API would be called")
}

Our last round of data validation testing:

query.census(geography = "State")

[1] "API would be called"

query.census(geography = "Region", specific_geo = 2)

[1] "API would be called"

query.census(geography = "ST 558", specific_geo = 2)

Error in query.census(geography = "ST 558", specific_geo = 2): Invalid geography classification. Specify one of: State, Region, Division

query.census(geography = "Division", specific_geo = 10)

Error in query.census(geography = "Division", specific_geo = 10): Invalid division requested. Specify a number 1 to 9 or `all`

query.census(geography = "Division", specific_geo = "all")

[1] "API would be called"


Looking good! Now that we know our input data is valid, we can build the URL and call the api from the function:

query.census <- function(year = 2022, numeric_vars = "AGEP", categorical_vars = "SEX", 
                         geography = "State", specific_geo = 3) {
  # check for valid year
  if(year == 2020 | !(year %in% 2010:2022)) stop("Error: Invalid year. Specify a year between 2010 and 2022 (not 2020)")
  
  # check for valid numeric vars
  numeric_vars_opts <- c("AGEP", "GASP", "GRPIP", "JWAP", "JWDP", "JWMNP")
  if(length(numeric_vars) == 0) stop("No numeric variable selected. Specify at least one.")
  for(i in numeric_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% numeric_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid numeric variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: AGEP, GASP, GRPIP, JWAP, JWDP, JWMNP")
  }
  
  numeric_vars <- c(numeric_vars, "PWGTP")
  
  # check for valid categorical vars
  categorical_vars_opts <- c("FER", "HHL", "SCH", "SCHL", "SEX")
  if(length(categorical_vars) == 0) stop("No categorical variable selected. Specify at least one or leave SEX as the default")
  for(i in categorical_vars) {
    if(!(i %in% categorical_vars_opts)) stop("Invalid categorical variable. Specify a single variable or a vector of variables. Options: FER, HHL, SCH, SCHL, SEX")
  }
  
  # check for valid geography scope and region of interest
  geography_opts <- c("Region", "State", "Division")
  if(!(geography %in% geography_opts)) stop("Invalid geography classification. Specify one of: State, Region, Division")
  if(specific_geo == "all") {
    specific_geo = "*"
  }
  else {
    if(geography == "State" & !(specific_geo %in% 1:50)) stop("Invalid state requested. Specify a number 1 to 50 or `all`")
    if(geography == "Region" & !(specific_geo %in% 1:4)) stop("Invalid region requested. Specify a number 1 to 4 or `all`")
    if(geography == "Division" & !(specific_geo %in% 1:9)) stop("Invalid division requested. Specify a number 1 to 9 or `all`")
  }
  
  api_key <- "59c3be738e0d051c63b57b6327de0b11ef0f7a1e"
  api_url <- paste0("https://api.census.gov/data/2022/acs/acs1/pums?get=",
                    paste0(numeric_vars, collapse = ","),
                    ",",
                    paste0(categorical_vars, collapse = ","),
                    "&for=",
                    geography,
                    ":",
                    specific_geo, 
                    "&key=",
                   api_key)
  
  return(api_url)
}

title: ST 558 Project 1
